items.title: Noa Lang gepresenteerd bij Galatasaray
items.summary: Noa Lang is speler van Galatasaray. Vrijdagavond heeft de Turkse topclub de komst van de linksbuiten aangekondigd via haar officiële kanalen.

items.title: Sources: Arsenal exploring Álvarez summer move
items.summary: Arsenal are exploring the possibility of launching a summer move for Atletico Madrid forward Julián Álvarez, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: German official calls for World Cup boycott talks
items.summary: A German soccer federation executive committee member says it's time to consider a World Cup boycott because of the actions of U.S. President Donald Trump.

items.title: Infantino: Resale will drive up WC ticket prices
items.summary: FIFA president Gianni Infantino said that many fans who have applied for tickets for the 2026 World Cup will be looking to sell them for profit on resale sites, thus driving up the cost of ticket prices.

items.title: Amerikaan Rodman wordt best betaalde speler ter wereld
items.summary: In dit blog volgen we alle ontwikkelingen in het vrouwenvoetbal. We besteden onder meer aandacht aan de Eurojackpot Vrouwen Eredivisie, de Europese toernooien en het Nederlands elftal.

items.title: Nottingham Forest presenteert Ajax-flop Lucca
items.summary: Lorenzo Lucca vervolgt zijn loopbaan in de Premier League. Vrijdag presenteerde Nottingham Forest de boomlange Italiaanse spits, die overkomt van Napoli.

items.title: Palace-coach Glasner bindt in na stevige kritiek: 'Na onweer komt zonneschijn'
items.summary: Crystal Palace-coach Oliver Glasner heeft zich richting een volgende wedstrijd wat schappelijker opgesteld na zijn tirade van vorige week. De Oostenrijker haalde vanwege het vertrek van zijn aanvoerder Marc Guéhi flink uit naar de clubleiding.

items.title: 'Barça jaagt op Nederlandse verdediger en beloont uitblinker met nieuw contract'
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Premier League Power Rankings: Rating all 20 teams on performances, not results
items.summary: In the debut of ESPN's Premier League Power Rankings, a look at all 20 teams, including why Liverpool, Chelsea and Tottenham are better than the table suggests.

items.title: Keep, Dump or Extend: How far will Chelsea go to back Liam Rosenior?
items.summary: Will Chelsea add cover at center back in January to support their new coach?

items.title: FA fine Chelsea for Villa bottle throwing incident
items.summary: Chelsea are fined £150,000 after an unidentified member of their staff threw a bottle towards Aston Villa's bench at the end of a Premier League match in December.

items.title: Billam-Smith: I'd love that Wilder fight!
items.summary: Chris Billam-Smith has plans for cruiserweight title fights, but would move up a division for a clash with huge-punching veteran Deontay Wilder.

items.title: Euros abuse made me not want to leave hotel - Carter
items.summary: England defender Jess Carter says abuse of female footballers is getting worse and that she has had to step back from social media after receiving racist messages during Euro 2025.

items.title: Then and now: what has changed since the opening Premier League games?
items.summary: This weekend’s fixtures are the reverse of a first round in which Nottingham Forest thrashed Brentford and there was optimism at Spurs Start-of-season shape: Continue reading...

items.title: Billionaire gambler Tony Bloom denies owing millions to former colleague
items.summary: Brighton & Hove Albion football club owner confirms placing bets through accounts of Reform UK adviser George Cottrell, according to legal documents The billionaire owner of Brighton & Hove Albion football club has confirmed his syndicate placed millions of pounds worth of bets through the gambling accounts of the Reform UK adviser George Cottrell. The admission comes in a document filed to the high court by Tony Bloom, who also admits that he, Cottrell and a former employee, Ryan Dudfield, had an agreement under which winnings were due to be split between them. Continue reading...

items.title: Palmer 'very, very happy' at Chelsea, says Rosenior
items.summary: Reports suggest Chelsea forward Cole Palmer is homesick amid links to Manchester United.

items.title: Glasner '100% committed' to Palace for rest of season
items.summary: Oliver Glasner says he is "100% committed" to Crystal Palace for the remainder of his time as manager of the club.

items.title: Hamilton 'massively excited' for 'new beginning' in F1 2026
items.summary: Lewis Hamilton says he is "massively excited" for "new beginnings" heading into Formula 1's latest rules era after he drove Ferrari's 2026 car for the first time.

items.title: Borthwick: England's prop crisis is disruptive - but we'll attack Six Nations
items.summary: England head coach Steve Borthwick has conceded to Sky Sports his squad's prop injury crisis is "unfortunate and disruptive" but says they will "attack the tournament" in search of a first Six Nations title since 2020.

items.title: McIlroy seven shots back as Reed leads at halfway stage in Dubai
items.summary: Rory McIlroy is seven shots adrift of leader Patrick Reed at the halfway stage of the Hero Dubai Desert Classic as he aims to win the event for a third time in four years.
